sourceMovie.title: Blow Out
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Blow Out (1981) stuck because it commits to its crime premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Brian De Palma's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The crime and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Chinatown
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Blow Out clicked for you, Chinatown (1974) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Blow Out?
faq.answer: Try Chinatown, The International, Klute—each shares DNA with Blow Out while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Blow Out?
faq.answer: Blow Out (1981) is best described as Crime, Mystery, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Blow Out worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
